info.name: Nukhba Alawael API
info.description:
Generated from the NestJS controllers. Global prefix is /api. Success responses are wrapped as { message, data }.

Auth: run Auth > Login first — a test script stores data.accessToken / data.refreshToken into collection variables, and every guarded request inherits Bearer {{accessToken}}.

Roles: admin (user/manage, school/manage, learning/admin, public-content writes), contentWriter (school/me tree), public (auth, files, learning metaData/tracks, public-content reads).

request.description: Role: admin. Clears the school logo.

item.name: School — owner (me)
item.description: Requires role: contentWriter (school owner). All routes under /api/school/me.

request.description: Role: contentWriter. Clears the owner school logo.

status: draft | published.

Publishing requires the lesson to already have at least one question, and a published lesson cannot then be emptied — set it back to draft to delete its last question.

formdata.description: Optional. Enable and pick a file to attach an image.

request.description:
Single create is multipart (form-data) — that is the only way to attach `image`.

Nested arrays use bracket notation (`options[0][text]`); multer parses it into a real array and the DTOs coerce the strings back to booleans/numbers.

`type`: options | trueFalse | match. `purpose` is REQUIRED: lesson | dailyChallenge.

Exactly one option must have isCorrect=true, minimum 2 options.

formdata.description: true | false — the whole answer key
Optional. Enable and pick a file to attach an image.

request.description: trueFalse carries its answer key in the single `correctAnswer` boolean; it is stored on the question as `trueOrFalseAnswer` and reads back there (no option rows). Send no options/matchingItems.

formdata.value: Match each animal to its sound

formdata.description: Optional. Enable and pick a file to attach an image.

request.description:
Minimum 3 items. `type`: base | match.

`correctIndex` is only on base rows and is a POSITION in this same array — it must point at a `match` row, each match can back only one base, and matches must be >= bases.

Here base "Cat"(2) -> index 0 = "Meow", base "Dog"(3) -> index 1 = "Bark".

formdata.description: Optional. Enable and pick a file to attach an image.

request.description: purpose=dailyChallenge takes `courseId` and NO `lessonId` — sending a lessonId is rejected. These live in the school pool rather than a lesson.

item.name: Bulk create questions (raw JSON, no files)

request.description:
Bulk create is raw JSON — no image support on this route.

All-or-nothing: one invalid question drops the whole batch (single transaction, everything validated before the first write).

`purpose` is required on every item, same as single create.

formdata.description: Optional. Leave disabled to keep the current image; enable and pick a file to swap it.

request.description:
Switch to / stay on `options`. `type` is sent, so the full `options[]` array is required and replaces the whole answer key.

---

Mirrors QuestionEditDto — every field is optional:
• `title` — optional, on its own or with anything else.
• `image` — optional. Omit to keep the current one, send a file to swap it.
• `type` — optional. **Sending it makes its components REQUIRED** (that is what @ValidateIf does): options -> `options[]` (min 2, exactly one isCorrect), trueFalse -> `correctAnswer`, match -> `matchingItems[]` (min 3).

The answer key is ATOMIC: send `type` + the full components and the old key is wiped and rebuilt. You cannot edit one option or one match row.

Omit `type` and the answer key is left untouched — any components you send are ignored.

formdata.description: Optional. Leave disabled to keep the current image; enable and pick a file to swap it.

request.description:
Switch to / stay on `trueFalse`. `type` is sent, so `correctAnswer` is required. Coming from options/match, the old rows are dropped; the key lands on the question as `trueOrFalseAnswer`.

---

Mirrors QuestionEditDto — every field is optional:
• `title` — optional, on its own or with anything else.
• `image` — optional. Omit to keep the current one, send a file to swap it.
• `type` — optional. **Sending it makes its components REQUIRED** (that is what @ValidateIf does): options -> `options[]` (min 2, exactly one isCorrect), trueFalse -> `correctAnswer`, match -> `matchingItems[]` (min 3).

The answer key is ATOMIC: send `type` + the full components and the old key is wiped and rebuilt. You cannot edit one option or one match row.

Omit `type` and the answer key is left untouched — any components you send are ignored.

formdata.description: Optional. Leave disabled to keep the current image; enable and pick a file to swap it.

request.description:
Switch to / stay on `match`. `type` is sent, so the full `matchingItems[]` array is required (min 3). `correctIndex` is a position in this same array and must point at a `match` row. Switching away resets `trueOrFalseAnswer` to null.

---

Mirrors QuestionEditDto — every field is optional:
• `title` — optional, on its own or with anything else.
• `image` — optional. Omit to keep the current one, send a file to swap it.
• `type` — optional. **Sending it makes its components REQUIRED** (that is what @ValidateIf does): options -> `options[]` (min 2, exactly one isCorrect), trueFalse -> `correctAnswer`, match -> `matchingItems[]` (min 3).

The answer key is ATOMIC: send `type` + the full components and the old key is wiped and rebuilt. You cannot edit one option or one match row.

Omit `type` and the answer key is left untouched — any components you send are ignored.

item.name: Update question — title / image, no type (form-data)

formdata.description: Optional. Leave disabled to keep the current image; enable and pick a file to swap it.

request.description:
No `type` sent, so the answer key survives exactly as-is. Use this to rename and/or swap the image without touching options/matchingItems/correctAnswer.

---

Mirrors QuestionEditDto — every field is optional:
• `title` — optional, on its own or with anything else.
• `image` — optional. Omit to keep the current one, send a file to swap it.
• `type` — optional. **Sending it makes its components REQUIRED** (that is what @ValidateIf does): options -> `options[]` (min 2, exactly one isCorrect), trueFalse -> `correctAnswer`, match -> `matchingItems[]` (min 3).

The answer key is ATOMIC: send `type` + the full components and the old key is wiped and rebuilt. You cannot edit one option or one match row.

Omit `type` and the answer key is left untouched — any components you send are ignored.

By id.

Rejected when the question is the LAST one of a `published` lesson — set the lesson to `draft` first. Also rejected while today's daily challenge uses it.

Deleting the whole lesson or unit tears its questions down regardless (guards are skipped there).

By ids. POST rather than DELETE because DELETE bodies get dropped by some proxies.

All-or-nothing: one undeletable question cancels the batch. Same guards as single delete.

item.name: Learning — admin
item.description: Requires role: admin. Read routes span all schools — no track-access checks.

request.description: Not paginated. Includes the track relation.
Paginated like the school route, but without track-access scoping.

body.raw:
{
  "googlePlay": "https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=app",
  "appStore": "https://apps.apple.com/app/id000000",
  "phone": "+963900000000",
  "location": "Damascus",
  "position": { "lat": 33.5138, "lng": 36.2765 },
  "about": "About the platform...",
  "privacyPolicy": "Privacy policy text...",
  "termsAndConditions": "Terms and conditions text..."
}

item.name: Daily wisement
item.description: Rotating daily wisement. Public: today. Admin: CRUD + bulk. The selected one cannot be edited or deleted; a midnight cron rotates the selection.

request.description: Returns the selected wisement; when none is selected yet it picks one on the spot.
All-or-nothing: one invalid item drops the whole batch.
Fails with 400 when the target is the currently selected wisement.
Fails with 400 when the target is the currently selected wisement.
All-or-nothing: a missing id or the currently selected wisement in the list fails the whole batch.

item.name: Student
item.description: One profile per student — a single school and track, never changed. Profiles are created by redeeming subscription keys. /student/school*: contentWriter (own school). /student*: admin.

request.description: Role: student. The single profile with school + track. 403 (StudentGuard) until the student has redeemed a subscription key.
Role: contentWriter. Pinned to the owner school. Paginated ({ list, totalRecords, skip, limit }) with user/school/track relations.
Role: contentWriter. A foreign profile reads as 404.
Role: contentWriter. Only the owning school toggles active. active=false blocks the student (StudentGuard -> 403 Student_2). Foreign profile reads as 404.
Role: admin. Spans all schools; paginated.
Role: admin.

item.name: Subscription
item.description: Subscription keys (6 groups: XXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX, single use). Subscribe: student. keys/school: school owner. keys CRUD: admin.

request.description: Role: student. Enrolls the student (or reuses their profile) and adds a paid subscription (expireDate = now + KEY_AGE_YEAR), consuming the key in one transaction. 400 while a live PAID subscription is still running (a live free trial can be upgraded), or when the key's school/track differ from the existing profile.
Role: student. One-shot free-trial subscription on the default school for the given track (expires after FREE_TRIAL_DAYS). 400 if the student has ever had any subscription.
Role: student. The caller's latest subscription (live or expired) so the client can show remaining time / prompt a renewal.
Role: admin. Paginated across every subscription; filter by userId, type, status.
Role: contentWriter. Pinned to the owner school. Paginated with school + track relations.
Role: admin. Spans all schools; paginated.
Role: admin. Mints `count` keys (1–500) for the school+track.
Role: admin.
Role: admin. All-or-nothing: any missing id cancels the whole batch.
